headlines.title: 173-year-old denim giant sees one fashion trend surge 70%
headlines.summary: Levi Strauss has spent more than 150 years convincing consumers that jeans belong in almost every wardrobe. Now, its next growth challenge is getting those shoppers to buy much more than denim bottoms.

headlines.title: Q2 Earnings Season Arrives: Can Results Match Upbeat Expectations?
headlines.summary: The big banks dominate this week's reporting docket, though several notable companies from other sectors will also report, including Netflix, Johnson & Johnson, and others. With the outlook trending consistently higher, will companies be able to live up to expectations?

headlines.title: It’s SK Hynix Stock’s Big Day. Just Look at Micron Shares.
headlines.summary: Micron Technology  stock played second fiddle to memory-chip peer  SK Hynix’s  strong market debut on Friday.  While Micron stock eked briefly into positive territory Friday, it closed lower as  SK Hynix  American depositary receipts shot above their initial public offering price of $149.  Micron stock closed down 1.2% at $979.30, while SK Hynix’s ADRs closed at $168.49 Friday, up 13% from its IPO price.

headlines.title: Meta Leads Again as Markets Look Forward to Earnings: Stock Market Today
headlines.summary: Every sector but healthcare was up on Friday, as investors, traders and speculators priced in broad optimism about the future.

headlines.title: Bank of America argues Amazon retail rival is major AI winner
headlines.summary: Online shopping is moving into a new phase, and consumers may not always notice who is powering it. A shopper today can ask an AI assistant to compare products, find deals, read reviews, build a cart, and move closer to checkout without visiting a traditional retailer's website.

description: Bank of America Corporation, through its subsidiaries, provides various financial products and services for individual consumers, small and middle-market businesses, institutional investors, large corporations, and governments worldwide. It operates through four segments: Consumer Banking, Global Wealth & Investment Management (GWIM), Global Banking, and Global Markets. The Consumer Banking segment offers traditional and money market savings accounts, certificates of deposit and IRAs, checking accounts, and investment accounts and products; credit and debit cards; residential mortgages and home equity loans; and direct and indirect loans. The GWIM segment provides investment management, brokerage, banking, and trust and retirement products and services; wealth management solutions; and customized solutions, including specialty asset management services. The Global Banking segment offers lending products and services, including commercial loans, leases, commitment facilities, trade finance, and commercial real estate and asset-based lending; treasury solutions, and underwriting and advisory services. The Global Markets segment provides market-making, financing, securities clearing, settlement, and custody services; securities and derivative products; and risk management products using interest rate, equity, credit, currency and commodity derivatives, foreign exchange, fixed-income, and mortgage-related products. Bank of America Corporation was founded in 1784 and is based in Charlotte, North Carolina.
